registryName: VANTARIS ONE Package Route Enforcement Registry
enforcementPrinciples: Menu visibility never grants API access.
Direct URL policy must equal menu policy.
Backend package enforcement is authoritative for business APIs.
installed, entitled, enabled, visible and healthy are independent.
Package registry membership alone is not runtime enforcement.
Locked packages may expose a governed lock page but no business API.
Disabled packages may expose admin diagnostics only.
Unavailable packages may expose historical and diagnostic views where safe.
Degraded packages may permit safe reads while restricting writes.
Mandatory packages still require installed and enabled runtime validation.
Package health does not automatically determine entitlement.
Optional packages require entitlement, installation and enablement.
External integration packages require compatibility and remote health checks.
Frontend fallback menus must use the same policy inputs.
Backend denial decisions must emit AuditRecord where identity is known.

packageStateSemantics.frontendUse: Select route, lock page or installation status.
Select authorized menu or governed lock page.
packageStateSemantics.backendUse: Deny optional-package business API when false.

packageStateSemantics.frontendUse: Hide, lock, or expose governed status according to policy.
Controls presentation only and never grants access.

packageStateSemantics.authoritativeProvider: UConsole readiness and package health provider
packageStateSemantics.frontendUse: Choose normal, degraded, unavailable, or status presentation.
packageStateSemantics.backendUse: Restrict operations according to health access semantics.

packageStateSemantics.frontendUse: Show compatibility state and upgrade guidance.
packageStateSemantics.backendUse: Deny writes and controls when incompatible.
Limit APIs to approved safe mode.
